title: dasystephana newberryi
intro: Dasystephana newberryi, also known as the Newberry's dasystephana or the desert lavender, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce infections. The health benefits of Dasystephana newberryi include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and possibly lowering blood pressure, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Dasystephana newberryi typically involve making teas or infusions from the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
